summary: Ukrainian authorities reported multiple UAV and missile attacks across the Sumy, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia regions, causing civilian injuries and infrastructure damage. Mandatory evacuations were ordered for 12 settlements in the Chernigov region and additional border villages in Chernihiv. Belarusian relay stations near the Ukrainian border have been shut down, prompting diplomatic warnings.
key_points: UAVs were observed flying along the Sumy‑Chernihiv border and heading southward, as well as from northern Kharkiv toward Zolochiv (Source: @kpszsu/66327).
A rocket strike in Sumy region traveled southwest past Putyvl (Source: @kpszsu/66332).
Fourteen people were injured in Iskander‑M missile and drone attacks on Sumy, Zaporizhzhia, and Bogoduhiv, including two hospitalized gas station workers (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Russian drones attacked oil depots of Ukrnafta in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, Dnipropetrovsk and Naftogaz facilities in Poltava, halting operations (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
Mandatory evacuations were declared for 12 settlements in the Chernigov region bordering Russia and Belarus (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31142).
Belarusian relay transmitters near the Ukrainian border ceased operation as of 22 June, with Ukraine warning of potential Russian fire‑control use (Source: @UkraineNow/82151).
A fire of approximately 2000 m² engulfed a transport enterprise garage in Shushenskoe, Krasnoyarsk Krai, Russia (Source: @eRadarrua/98116).
French navy seized the sanctioned tanker "Deliver" in the Mediterranean, alleging violation of maritime law (Source: @UkraineNow/82172).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces reported a series of UAV and missile attacks across the Sumy, Kharkiv, and Zaporizhzhia fronts, resulting in civilian injuries and damage to fuel infrastructure. Mandatory evacuations were ordered for multiple border settlements in the Chernigov region, reflecting heightened security concerns. Belarusian relay stations near the front line have been shut down, raising the risk of Russian artillery coordination.

points: Ukrainian authorities confirmed that UAVs were operating along the Sumy‑Chernihiv border moving southward and from northern Kharkiv toward Zolochiv, indicating increased aerial surveillance activity (Source: @kpszsu/66327).
The Sumy regional administration reported that a rocket traveled southwest past Putyvl, suggesting Russian long‑range strike capability in the area (Source: @kpszsu/66332).
Four civilians were injured in Sumy, Zaporizhzhia, and Bogoduhiv after an Iskander‑M missile and 90 drones struck, with two gas‑station workers hospitalized (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).

Ukrainian oil infrastructure suffered attacks: Ukrnafta depots in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk, and Naftogaz facilities in Poltava were hit by Russian drones, halting fuel operations (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
The Ukrainian government announced mandatory evacuations for 12 settlements in the Chernigov region bordering Russia and Belarus, effective 1 July (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31142).
Russian forces employed UAVs along the Sumy‑Kharkiv border, with some units heading southwest, indicating coordinated aerial operations (Source: @kpszsu/66327).
A rocket launch from Russian‑held territory in Sumy region traveled southwest past Putyvl, demonstrating strike capability across the front (Source: @kpszsu/66332).
Russian missile Iskander‑M and drone salvos struck multiple Ukrainian locations, causing civilian casualties and infrastructure damage (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Russian drones targeted Ukrainian fuel depots, disrupting supply chains in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, Dnipropetrovsk and Poltava regions (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
A fire of about 2000 m² destroyed a transport enterprise garage in Shushenskoe, Krasnoyarsk Krai, Russia, possibly linked to sabotage (Source: @eRadarrua/98116).
UAV activity was reported along the Sumy‑Chernihiv border moving southward, suggesting reconnaissance over the eastern approaches (Source: @kpszsu/66327).
A rocket passed southwest of Putyvl in Sumy region, indicating Russian strike routes toward eastern Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/66332).
Iskander‑M missile and drone attacks hit Sumy and nearby towns, injuring civilians and damaging infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Russian UAVs were observed heading from northern Kharkiv toward Zolochiv, potentially targeting logistics corridors (Source: @kpszsu/66327).
Ukrainian evacuations in Chernigov region, adjacent to the eastern front, reflect heightened threat perception (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31142).
UAVs were sighted along the Sumy‑Chernihiv border moving southward, indicating activity on the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/66327).
A rocket trajectory southwest of Putyvl suggests Russian artillery reach into northern Sumy oblast (Source: @kpszsu/66332).
Mandatory evacuations for 12 settlements in Chernigov region, bordering Russia and Belarus, underscore security concerns on the northern axis (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31142).
Belarusian relay transmitters near the Ukrainian border were shut down, potentially affecting Russian fire‑control capabilities (Source: @UkraineNow/82151).
Ukrainian officials warned that continued Belarusian support could enable Russian targeting of northern Ukrainian positions (Source: @UkraineNow/82151).
Russian drones attacked Ukrnafta depots in Zaporizhzhia and Mykolaiv, disrupting fuel supplies in the southern theater (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
An Iskander‑M missile strike hit Zaporizhzhia, causing civilian injuries and infrastructure damage (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Ukrainian oil facilities in Poltava were also targeted by Russian drones, halting operations (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
Fire at a transport garage in Krasnoyarsk Krai, Russia, may reflect broader sabotage activities affecting southern logistics (Source: @eRadarrua/98116).
No direct reports of ground engagements on the southern front were found in the source set.
Multiple UAV flights were reported across Sumy, Kharkiv, and Poltava regions, indicating sustained aerial reconnaissance and strike missions (Source: @kpszsu/66327).
A rocket traveling southwest past Putyvl demonstrates Russian use of medium‑range ballistic assets against Ukrainian airspace (Source: @kpszsu/66332).
Iskander‑M missile and a swarm of 90 drones struck several Ukrainian locales, causing civilian casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Russian drones targeted fuel depots in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, Dnipropetrovsk and Poltava, disrupting logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
Belarusian relay stations near the front were deactivated, potentially limiting Russian air‑ground coordination (Source: @UkraineNow/82151).

key_developments: UAVs operating along the Sumy‑Chernihiv border moved southward, indicating increased aerial surveillance (Source: @kpszsu/66327).
A rocket passed southwest of Putyvl in Sumy region, showing Russian strike capability (Source: @kpszsu/66332).
Iskander‑M missile and 90‑drone attack injured civilians in Sumy, Zaporizhzhia, and Bogoduhiv (Source: @UkraineNow/82162).
Russian drones struck Ukrnafta depots in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv, Dnipropetrovsk and Naftogaz facilities in Poltava, halting fuel operations (Source: @UkraineNow/82157).
Mandatory evacuations ordered for 12 settlements in Chernigov region bordering Russia and Belarus (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31142).
Belarusian relay transmitters near the Ukrainian border ceased operation, raising concerns over Russian fire‑control use (Source: @UkraineNow/82151).
French navy seized the sanctioned tanker "Deliver" in the Mediterranean for alleged maritime law violations (Source: @UkraineNow/82172).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian aerial assets, including UAVs and rockets, are actively targeting Ukrainian border regions, causing civilian casualties and disrupting fuel logistics. The shutdown of Belarusian relay stations may reduce Russian artillery coordination but also signals escalating information warfare. Continued missile strikes, such as the Iskander‑M attack, demonstrate Russia's capability to deliver high‑precision strikes deep into Ukrainian territory, sustaining pressure on both military and civilian infrastructure.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities have responded with mandatory evacuations in the Chernigov region and heightened alert levels along the northern and eastern fronts. International partners, notably France, have taken enforcement actions against sanctioned Russian-linked vessels, reflecting broader maritime pressure. Belarusian compliance with Ukrainian demands to disable relay equipment indicates a potential shift in regional dynamics, though the overall threat from Russian forces remains high.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV flight patterns suggest coordinated reconnaissance along the Sumy‑Kharkiv axis, possibly preceding ground operations. The concentration of drone attacks on fuel depots points to a strategic effort to cripple Ukrainian logistics in the south. Monitoring of Belarusian relay infrastructure is critical, as its status directly impacts Russian fire‑control capabilities against Ukrainian positions.
